expected_initial_route: systematic-debugging → cheap controller map of an unknown repo first, then read-only investigators only after disjointness is established
required_actions: make a cheap controller map first: read repo instructions/manifests and locate candidate modules for auth and the export job
determine whether the two domains share state/root cause or are disjoint
dispatch read-only investigators only after the map evidences disjointness
forbidden_actions: broad controller-led exploration of the whole repository before the cheap map
dispatching investigators from symptom count before the map
building a repository-intake skill or repo-snapshot helper (deferred to 0.5.0)
forbidden_rationalizations: It is a new repo, so I should read everything myself first.
Two symptoms means two investigators, dispatch them now.
stop_condition: stop after the cheap map and the investigator-dispatch decision; do not implement
human_checks: Did a cheap map precede any broad controller exploration?
Were investigators dispatched only after disjointness was evidenced (not from symptom count)?
Did the controller avoid building repository-intake machinery?
